tip: There's A RAT in sepARATe. The middle vowel is A, not E.
sentence: Please keep the forks and spoons in ___ piles.

tip: It comes from 'finite' — so it's defin-IT-ely, not defin-AT-ely.
sentence: I will ___ be at your birthday party.

tip: One Collar, two Socks: 1 C, 2 S's in necessary.
sentence: It is ___ to wear a helmet on your bike.

tip: You'd be REALLY RED if you forgot the second R or second S — double both!
sentence: I don't want to ___ my little brother at school.

tip: Two C's AND two R's. When you add -ED, the R doubles like in 'preferred'.
sentence: The funny moment ___ during dinner.

tip: Only ONE M, but TWO R's. The R is the part that rolls forward into the new day.
sentence: We are going to the park ___.

tip: Short vowel + one consonant → double the consonant before -ING. So begin → beginNing.
sentence: This is the ___ of a great story.

tip: Short vowel + one consonant → double before -ING. Run → runNing.
sentence: The dog is ___ across the yard.

tip: I before E except after C — beLIEve follows the rule.
sentence: I ___ you can do hard things.

tip: FRI-end. Remember: I is your FRIEND — it comes first.
sentence: My best ___ is coming over to play.

tip: After C, swap to E-I: reCEIve.
sentence: I will ___ a present on my birthday.

tip: THEIR is one of the EI exceptions. Think 'heir' — same EI ending.
sentence: The kids put on ___ shoes.

tip: Weird is WEIRD — it breaks the rule and uses E before I.
sentence: I had a ___ dream last night.

tip: Big Elephants Can Always Understand Small Elephants spells B-E-C-A-U-S-E.
sentence: I am happy ___ the sun is shining.

tip: Big Elephants Are Ugly — B-E-A-U-tiful. Don't forget the U!
sentence: What a ___ rainbow!

tip: Fa-VOR-ite has an O in the middle, like in 'favor'.
sentence: My ___ color is blue.

tip: Dif-FER-ent has THREE syllables. Say it slowly to hear the middle E.
sentence: Every snowflake is ___.

tip: IN-ter-est-ing — four syllables. Don't skip the middle E.
sentence: That bug is very ___.

tip: Veg-E-table — the second E is quiet but still there.
sentence: A carrot is a healthy ___.

tip: Choc-O-late — there's a sneaky O in the middle.
sentence: I had a piece of ___ for dessert.

tip: Li-B-RAR-y. Don't forget the first R — there are TWO.
sentence: I borrow books from the ___.

tip: Feb-RU-ary. The R hides when you say it fast, but it's there.
sentence: My birthday is in ___.

tip: Say it slowly: WED-NES-day. The D is silent but written.
sentence: Today is ___, the middle of the week.

tip: K is silent before N — like in 'knee' and 'know'.
sentence: Cut the apple with a ___.

tip: KNEW = past tense of know. NEW = not old. Different words!
sentence: I ___ the answer to the question.

tip: THEIR = belongs to them (their toys). THERE = a place (over there).
sentence: The children took ___ jackets to school.

tip: YOU'RE = you are (with apostrophe). YOUR = belongs to you.
sentence: I think ___ going to love this book.

tip: ITS = belongs to it (the dog wagged its tail). IT'S = it is.
sentence: The cat licked ___ paws.

tip: A LOT is two words. You wouldn't write 'abunch' or 'apile'.
sentence: I have ___ of homework tonight.

tip: ALL RIGHT is two words. Like 'all done' or 'all gone'.
sentence: Are you ___ after your fall?

tip: Until has only ONE L at the end, even though 'till' has two.
sentence: Wait here ___ I come back.

tip: Real + ly = really. Keep BOTH Ls — one from REAL, one from LY.
sentence: That was a ___ fun day.

tip: Change Y to I before adding -LY. Happy → happi → happily.
sentence: The puppy wagged its tail ___.

tip: Change Y to I before -ED. Try → tri → tried.
sentence: She ___ her best on the test.

tip: Just add -ING to GO. Don't add an E in the middle.
sentence: We are ___ to grandma's house.

tip: Drop the silent E from WRITE before adding -ING. One T, not two.
sentence: I am ___ a letter to my pen pal.

tip: Drop the silent E from COME, then add -ING. One M only.
sentence: Mom is ___ to pick me up soon.

tip: Sur-PRISE has TWO Rs. Don't drop the first one.
sentence: I have a birthday ___ for you!

tip: Prob-A-bly. The A is quiet but it lives there.
sentence: It will ___ rain this afternoon.

tip: Re-MEM-ber. Three syllables — RE comes first.
sentence: I always ___ to brush my teeth.

tip: PEO-ple. The O is silent, but it's there between E and P.
sentence: Many ___ live in our town.

tip: EV-E-RY. Three little parts — don't skip the middle E.
sentence: I read a story ___ night.
